title: Astracantha eschkerensis

intro: Astracantha eschkerensis, also known as Scorpion's Tail or Carob Milk Thistle, is a species of spiny shrub in the pea family (Fabaceae) native to the Mediterranean region. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and hepatoprotective properties, which may aid in reducing liver damage and promoting overall well-being. The bioactive constituents of Astracantha eschkerensis include saponins, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Astracantha eschkerensis is commonly used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and capsules, often combined with other herbs like milk thistle (Silybum marianum) to enhance its hepatoprotective effects.
